original_abstract: Designing Pb-free relaxors with both a high capacitive energy density (Wrec) and high storage efficiency (η) remains a remarkable challenge for cutting-edge pulsed power technologies. Local compositional heterogeneity is crucial for achieving complex polar structure in solid solution relaxors, but its role in optimizing energy storage properties is often overlooked. Here, we report that an exceptionally high Wrec of 15.2 J cm-3 along with an ultrahigh η of 91% can be achieved through designing local chemical clustering in Bi0.5Na0.5TiO3-BaTiO3-based relaxors. A three-dimensional atomistic model derived from neutron/X-ray total scattering combined with reverse Monte Carlo method reveals the presence of subnanometer scale clustering of Bi, Na, and Ba, which host differentiated polar displacements, and confirming the prediction by density functional theory calculations. This leads to a polar state with small polar clusters and strong length and direction fluctuations in unit-cell polar vectors, thus manifesting improved high-field polarizability, steadily reduced hysteresis, and high breakdown strength macroscopically. The favorable polar structure features also result in a unique field-increased η, excellent stability, and superior discharge capacity. Our work demonstrates that the hidden local chemical order exerts a significant impact on the polarization characteristic of relaxors, and can be exploited for accessing superior energy storage performance.
humanized_abstract: The realization of Pb-free relaxors with high capacitive energy density (W rec ) and high energy storage efficiency (η) is still a great challenge to the development of advanced pulsed power systems. Even though local compositional heterogeneity is necessary for generating complex polar structure in solid solution relaxors, studies on its role on energy storage properties are limited. In this work, we for the first time identified that designing local chemical clustering can lead to high energy storage efficiency and large energy density in relaxor materials. The values of W rec and η for a designed Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -BaTiO 2 -BaTiO 3 relaxor composition were measured as high as 15.2 J cm -3 and 91%, respectively. Combining the information derived from three-dimensional atomistic model constructed by neutron/X-ray total scattering and the reverse Monte Carlo method, the existence of subnanometer scale clustering of Bi, Na and Ba is evidenced, which have different polar displacements as confirmed by density functional theory calculations. All these lead to the realization of polar structure with small polar clusters and strong length and direction fluctuations in the unit-cell polar vectors, thus demonstrating the improved high-field polarizability, reduced hysteresis and high breakdown strength. In addition, the favorable polar structure features in the relaxor material also demonstrated a unique field-increased η, excellent stability and discharge capacity.
